title: Accepted behavior preservation should be a Registry-tracked Workbench capability
observedFailure: Later Capture Back overlays changed site/game1/index.html and regressed accepted runtime behavior such as group-filtered tap menus and hit target behavior.
acceptedLesson: Accepted behaviors must become durable behavior contracts and future CB overlays must preserve them or explicitly deprecate them with user approval.
recommendedProductUpdates: Add accepted capability tracking to the Registry dashboard.
Require CB impact analysis for files tied to accepted capabilities.
Add behavior-preservation ledgers to CB artifacts.
Support behavior verifiers in addition to file/token verifiers.
Aggregate repo-local product feedback signals across all registered Workbenches.
